rulings.Case Title: ROGER ROSS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 15, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 1. Defendant Schneider Electric USA¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ARTURO CRUZ VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Schneider Electric Usa, Inc. F/K/A Square D Company¿S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 15, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 6. Defendant Schneider Electric USA¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether any Square D products plaintiff worked with contained asbestos and whether any exposure to such products was a substantial factor in causing plaintiff¿s asbestos-related injury. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DELBERT JOHN MOLLESON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 15, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 2. Defendant Simpson Timber Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied denied as to negligence and strict liability (issues 1, 2, 4 and 5) and taken off calendar as to civil battery, false representation and punitive damages (issues 3, 6 and 7). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Delbert Molleson was exposed to asbestos-containing materials attributable to defendant. Although plaintiffs¿ responses to contention discovery identified Phillip Molleson as a person with knowledge of Delbert Molleson¿s exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant, defendant¿s separate statement did not refer to Phillip Molleson, much less make any showing that Phillip Molleson does not have such knowledge. Moreover, although referred to in defendant¿s reply memorandum, the issue of substantial factor causation (as opposed to product identification causation) was not mentioned in defendant¿s separate statement, so it is not a basis to grant defendant¿s motion. Defendant¿s evidentiary objection 4 and 8 are overruled. Plaintiffs¿ civil battery, false representation and punitive damages claims have been dismissed. = (220/HEK)
